parsed_review.summary: CP00-812 descriptor-only enterprise-SaaS failure-recovery secondary-workflow bridge pack (risk B, 40 units, RP26.P07.M03.S21-RP26.P07.M04.S30: Primary Implementation Slice tail rows + Secondary Workflow Slice rows) verified by read-only source inspection only (Read/Grep/Glob; no Bash, no command execution). Binding (registry.js:460), requirements/deliverable counts (registry.js:2152; claude_review 2 / implementation 19 / failure_recovery 12 / security_audit 2 / hermes_evidence 2 / fixture 1 / test 2 = 40, matching the prompt baseline and micro split 10/30), descriptor + case set (service.js:1737, createCaseSet:300, freezeResult:65 stamping ENTERPRISE_SAAS_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION on every row), Hermes/Claude/handoff packets (service.js:2074/2719/2994), validators (validators.js:142/178/779 with real boundary enforcement), tests (model.test.js:1045-1116), persisted contract (latest_pack/latest_projection=CP00-812, validation.valid=true at contract:70/35611/43874), live plan (closeout-pack-plan.json:398 with identical source_ledger_sha 13c7ed65), README (#cp00-812 README.md:67) and index.js export * all reconcile exactly. Clean non-overlapping continuation from CP00-811 (next_subphase RP26.P07.M05.S01 -> CP00-813). No-write/no-real-data/runtime-closed boundaries are enforced at attestation, row, descriptor, validator, test, and contract layers (silent_success, real_tenant_data, failure_recovery/incident runtime, executes_api_handler/ui_runtime, writes_product_state/permission_decision, audit_event_body, Hermes runtime receipts, SSO/SCIM/secret payloads, cross-tenant routes, enterprise_trust all closed). Claude is read-only/non-final (promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false; P0/P1/P2 block the pack); Hermes is descriptor-only evidence (receipt_shape descriptor_only_enterprise_saas_no_write, emits_runtime_receipt=false), not embedded product runtime. No over-claim: manifest reports production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, in_progress, review_pending with empty receipts. Review inputs are valid hardened evidence (auth preflight passed exit 0 / 1455B / valid JSON; model claude-opus-4-8, effort max, read_only, tools Read,Grep,Glob with no forbidden tools; schema present; summary-only correctly prohibited for risk B; prompt 2503B < 25600B and contains no full diff/source). No P0/P1/P2 found. Two non-blocking P3s: (1) the expected pre-closeout absence of downstream artifacts (command-evidence.json, claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) plus the fact that the listed validation commands were not and cannot be executed by this read-only reviewer (mirrors CP00-809/810/811); (2) a benign changed-file-scope dual-listing. PASS_WITH_FINDINGS; does not block pack closeout or this CP's closeout goal. Enterprise-trust/runtime opening remains deferred to human final approval, outside this receipt.

findings.title: Downstream closeout artifacts and validation-command evidence not yet present (expected at the Claude-review stage)
findings.details: manifest.required_pack_artifacts (lines 527-533) lists command-evidence.json, claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, but Glob of docs/closeout-packs/cp00-812/ confirms only manifest.json currently exists, and artifacts/closeout-pack-claude-review/cp00-812/ has no raw-output.json/review-receipt.json yet. pack_level_claude_review.status=review_pending with valid_review_receipts=[] and invalid_review_attempts=[] (manifest:517-526). This is the expected state during the Claude review step: claude-review-result.json wraps this review's verdict and adjudication.md is generated from the review receipt, so neither can exist before this review; command-evidence.json and construction-inspection.json plus execution of the listed validation commands (npm run closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) are produced/run by the closeout pipeline after this review. Being read-only (Read/Grep/Glob, no Bash), I verified by static inspection only: binding (registry.js:460), requirements/rows (registry.js:2152), descriptor + createCaseSet no-write attestation and authority boundary (service.js:1737, 300, 65; registry.js:460/477), validators (validators.js:142/178/779/793), CP812 tests (model.test.js:1045-1116), contract promotion/projection/validation.valid and guards (contract:70/35611/43874), live plan entry with matching source_ledger_sha (closeout-pack-plan.json:398), README (#cp00-812 README.md:67), and index.js export* re-exports; I did not and cannot execute the validation commands or confirm downstream adjudication/inspection output. The manifest correctly reports production_ready=false / runtime_ready=false / in_progress and does not overclaim runtime or enterprise trust. This mirrors the equivalent non-blocking P3 recorded for CP00-809/810/811.
findings.recommendation: Proceed with the normal closeout pipeline: run the listed validation commands, capture command-evidence.json, and generate claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json; then obtain human final approval before any runtime or enterprise-trust opening (deferred beyond CP00-812 / RP26.P07.M05.S01). No code change required for this pack.

findings.title: changed-file-scope dual-lists new-session-handoff.md as both untracked implementation and unrelated-preserved (benign, not part of pack)
findings.details: docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md appears in both untracked_implementation_files (line 160) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (line 166) of changed-file-scope.json. The authoritative active set is unambiguous and excludes it: active_pack_required_untracked_files lists only docs/closeout-packs/cp00-812 (line 14-16), active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty (line 163), and the manifest worktree_transition_policy.active_pack_required_untracked_files (manifest:498-501) lists only docs/closeout-packs/cp00-812 and artifacts/closeout-pack-claude-review/cp00-812. Per the prompt's instruction not to infer unrelated dirty files belong to this pack, new-session-handoff.md is correctly treated as NOT part of CP00-812; the dual classification is a conservative heuristic overlap in the scope-report generator (it flags any untracked docs/*.md as a candidate implementation file while still preserving it as unrelated). No impact on pack scope, boundaries, or closeout correctness.
findings.recommendation: Optionally tighten the changed-file-scope generator so a file preserved as unrelated is not simultaneously emitted under untracked_implementation_files, to avoid ambiguity in future review packets. No action required for CP00-812 closeout.
